claim: Antiretroviral therapy reduces rates of tuberculosis across a broad range of CD4 strata.

result.claim: Antiretroviral therapy reduces rates of tuberculosis across a broad range of CD4 strata.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from a systematic review and meta-analysis (Paper ID: 4883040). This paper explicitly states that "Antiretroviral therapy is strongly associated with a reduction in the incidence of tuberculosis across all CD4 count strata." The study further provides hazard ratios for different CD4 count categories (less than 200 cells/µl, 200 to 350 cells/µl, greater than 350 cells/µl, and any CD4 count), all indicating a significant reduction in tuberculosis incidence with antiretroviral therapy. The methodology of a systematic review and meta-analysis lends high credibility to these findings.

evidence.text: In 2010, there were an estimated 1.1 million incident cases of tuberculosis among the 34 million people living with HIV worldwide.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: Antibiotics can reduce colonization resistance against pathogens including Clostridium difficile.
evidence.source: Antibiotic-induced shifts in the mouse gut microbiome and metabolome increase susceptibility to Clostridium difficile infection

evidence.text: Prospective cohort studies were included if they compared tuberculosis incidence by antiretroviral therapy status in HIV-infected adults.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: The WHO guidelines recommended that all people living with HIV initiate antiretroviral therapy (ART) upon diagnosis, regardless of CD4 count.
evidence.source: The HIV Treatment Gap: Estimates of the Financial Resources Needed versus Available for Scale-Up of Antiretroviral Therapy in 97 Countries from 2015 to 2020

evidence.text: Clinically relevant doses of DNA methyltransferase and histone deacetylase inhibitors (DNMTi and HDACi) reduce the immune suppressive microenvironment through type I IFN signaling.
evidence.source: Epigenetic therapy activates type I interferon signaling in murine ovarian cancer to reduce immunosuppression and tumor burden.

evidence.text: Rifampicin and pyrazinamide efficiently penetrate the sites of TB infection in lung lesions.
evidence.source: The association between sterilizing activity and drug distribution into tuberculosis lesions

evidence.text: Moxifloxacin is active in vitro against a subpopulation of Mycobacterium tuberculosis that persists in specific niches under drug pressure.
evidence.source: The association between sterilizing activity and drug distribution into tuberculosis lesions

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cell–induced IICs and viral control are optimal when memory cells are either T helper type 1 (TH1) or TH17 polarized.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: The T helper 1 (Th1) cell cytokine IFN-gamma induces autophagy in macrophages to eliminate Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
evidence.source: T helper 2 cytokines inhibit autophagic control of intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Hyperimmune activation contributes to the decline in CD4+ T cell numbers during HIV infection.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: The adjusted hazards ratios for an increase in CD4 cells of 100 counts/microl or more was 1.15 (1.06 to 1.24).
evidence.source: Stable partnership and progression to AIDS or death in HIV infected patients receiving highly active antiretroviral therapy: Swiss HIV cohort study.

evidence.text: Randomised controlled trials were included if they compared tuberculosis incidence by antiretroviral therapy status in HIV-infected adults.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: Retrospective cohort studies were included if they compared tuberculosis incidence by antiretroviral therapy status in HIV-infected adults.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: The 90-90-90 treatment target was for 81% of people living with HIV on ART by 2020.
evidence.source: The HIV Treatment Gap: Estimates of the Financial Resources Needed versus Available for Scale-Up of Antiretroviral Therapy in 97 Countries from 2015 to 2020

evidence.text: The major objective of tuberculosis (TB) drug development is finding new treatment-shortening antibiotics to improve cure rates and curb the emergence of drug resistance.
evidence.source: The association between sterilizing activity and drug distribution into tuberculosis lesions

evidence.text: After 12 months, the deficiencies in CD4(+) T cell diversity had improved.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: Antiretroviral therapy is strongly associated with a reduction in the incidence of tuberculosis across all CD4 count strata.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: Antiretroviral therapy is associated with a reduction in the incidence of tuberculosis in all baseline CD4 count categories.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: There were four categories based on CD4 counts at antiretroviral therapy initiation for the meta-analyses.
evidence.source: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

evidence.text: 69 of 123 patients (56%) receiving capecitabine reported improvement in global QoL.
evidence.source: Chemotherapy options in elderly and frail patients with metastatic colorectal cancer (MRC FOCUS2): an open-label, randomised factorial trial
